description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a large, convex glass screen encased in a beige plastic frame. The front panel features a speaker grille, rotary dials, and push buttons for manual control, all set within a rectangular wooden cabinet with a rich, reddish-brown finish. The sides of the cabinet are smooth wood, with one side displaying a black ventilation grille. The back of the television is covered by a greenish, marbled plastic panel with multiple ventilation slots and a central bulge, likely for housing the cathode ray tube and electronics. The television stands on four short, square legs, elevating it slightly above the ground. The overall design is boxy and robust, characteristic of mid-20th-century home appliances.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a convex glass screen, wooden cabinet, and manual control dials on the front.
description_view_0: The left side view shows the smooth wooden panel and the protruding greenish back cover of the television.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the convex glass screen, beige frame, speaker grille, and manual control dials and buttons.
description_view_2: The right side view displays the wooden panel with a black ventilation grille and the greenish back cover.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the marbled green plastic back panel with ventilation slots and a central bulge.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick beige plastic bezel, rectangular, with rounded inner corners and a matte finish.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and push buttons on the lower right of the front panel.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
